finding: Audio rendering progress does not establish that audible output reached the intended hardware route. Web Audio 1.1 exposes browser-visible output-selection evidence through AudioContext.sinkId, setSinkId(), sinkchange, and speaker-selection policy checks, but even successful selection remains weaker than physical speaker or listener evidence.
design_implication: Pair render-position evidence with an output-routing classification. Treat unsupported, policy-blocked, default-only, selected-but-unverified, and browser-confirmed selection as distinct states. Never convert browser-visible route selection into a claim that sound was physically emitted or perceived.

verification.environment: isolated Node runtime using byte-identical pre-commit files

uncertainty: The W3C document is a draft and browser implementations may differ.
Feature presence does not prove permission, successful routing, hardware emission, or perception.
A sinkchange event confirms completion of the browser algorithm, not the physical acoustic path.
falsification_route: Run the classifier in target browsers and compare against actual API surfaces.
Select a non-default output and compare sink identity plus sinkchange with operating-system routing.
Pair browser-visible routing evidence with an external microphone, device-level meter, or listener report.
privacy: No private chat, health data, credentials, device identifiers, or personal information included.
